title: VILAS: A VLA-Integrated Low-cost Architecture with Soft 3D-Printed End-Effector for End-to-End Manipulation
abstract: We present VILAS, a fully low-cost, modular robotic manipulation platform designed to support end-to-end vision-language-action (VLA) policy learning and deployment on accessible hardware. The system integrates a 6-DOF arm, a 3D-printed soft end-effector, and a tri-camera vision setup, all of which can be assembled with a BOM under USD 1500. We design a complete software stack covering teleoperation-based data collection, policy fine-tuning, and real-world inference, fully compatible with OpenVLA, Pi-0 and Isaac GR00T checkpoints. On 70 self-collected manipulation tasks, fine-tuning OpenVLA-7B with VILAS achieves an average success rate of 81.3% — competitive with state-of-the-art platforms costing 6-20× more. Source code, hardware design files (CAD/STL), and a 2.5M-frame teleoperation dataset are released.
key_contributions: **完整开源工作流**: 第一个公开整合 "硬件 BOM ($1500) + 数据采集软件 + VLA 微调代码 + 部署 runtime" 的端到端低成本平台。
**3D 打印软体末端**: 取代传统二指夹爪，用 3D 打印 + 硅胶气动结构实现软体抓取，对易损物体（杯子/水果/电子元件）零损坏率。
**OpenVLA / Pi-0 / GR00T 三大开放 VLA 全部兼容**: 提供 LoRA fine-tune 脚本与 calibration utilities；70 任务平均成功率 81.3%。
**70 任务桌面操作数据集**: 250 万帧 RGB+触觉+语言指令，已上传 Hugging Face Datasets，可作为 ALOHA / SO-100 数据集补充。

experiments.benchmark: 易损物体抓取（杯子/水果）
experiments.result: 0% 损坏率（vs 硬夹爪 ≈ 17% 损坏率）
